intro: Haumaniastrum speciosum is a plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the broomrape family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Himalayas and the mountainous regions of China, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Haumaniastrum speciosum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess significant pharmacological activities.
